item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to be honest, I never thought about what would happen if you tried that. Thinking about it now, I think that if you fast forwarded or rewound the progress bar at the bottom would change accordingly but nothing would happen in the video itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very good point, I'll clarify that.

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
So, to start off, I don't like that we don't even get to know what the anomalous effect is until the second paragraph of the description. Ideally, I like to see the actual effect in the first couple sentences. I'm also unsure about the formatting in the log, in that both italic text in dialogue and separate lines are used to show actions, which is unnecessarily confusing.

Apart from that, I'm not really a massive fan of the content of the video transcript. It begins with a possibly interesting aspect, with the development of sapience and how that could affect something. However, after this it descends into fairly generic torture horror, only thematically connected through the use of mirrors and light. The back-and-forth of the beginning is lost to walls of text that I almost had to skim, and the body horror is too surreal to the point where I took too long working out what was happening for it to have any noticeable effect on me as a reader. The line about checking out his reflection felt forced, and it just added to my overall feeling of disappointment through the piece.

Overall, I feel like this began with an interesting premise, but the quick descent into non-relatable body horror damaged my experience and led me to downvote.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to gee0765 or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The paragraph that starts as "As the mirror does not take up the entire space of the recording, parts of the area behind the mirror are visible in the periphery of the frame…." was very confusing to me for the reason that I always automatically conceptualize the concept of a mirror as being mounted on a wall or piece of furniture, with no "area behind the mirror" possible.

I would suggest establishing that the mirror is free-standing, or not mounted on a wall or something so that the existence of an area behind the mirror can be possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the heck

+1

Also, I'm curious what would happen if you used video controls? Since most software used to play .mp4 files has pausing, fast forwarding, skipping, etc.

Personally I think I would be even more curious without the whole "Eldritch eyeball body-horror" ending, but I mean it works fine now. I also found it just a little bit difficult to envision what exactly was happening at the end so that may be a primary reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've notice a grammar mistake during the interview. It says "The reflection makes another hand gestures" which is not right. I suggest that you correct that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for bringing the spacing to my attention. It was spaced out in my draft, but for some reason it didn't copy over. I've fixed it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It managed to grab me by the existential horror, but then it wandered to more absurd surreal horror and I'm just thrown right out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy and good, I loved the realization that something was being created or gaining salience through the interactions with the skip.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I haven't read a scp in a while and this was fun, I think the log could use a space after each playback though, they're kinda squished together and it makes things kinda difficult to read.

1+

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Welcome, one and all, to my 5th SCP.

This was inspired by a reddit comment I saw in which someone described that as a young kid, they were confused by the fact that they couldn't see their reflection in a photo of a mirror. The idea developed into this.

I am a firm believer in Death of the Author and that there is no canon, you're interpretation of what's going on here is just as valid as mine. However, if you really want to know my understanding of what I wrote…

Spoilers
- close

Every time someone watches the video, something learns how to be self-aware. And whatever it is, it learned by their example.

- close

Big McThankies from McSpankies to margsentiff, Uncle Nicolini, and Kybard for their valuable and insightful crit.

My author page.

The image was made by me, combining and modifying this:
https://www.publicdomainpictures.net/en/view-image.php?image=70677&picture=cartoon-eyes
and this:
https://www.publicdomainpictures.net/en/view-image.php?image=238399&picture=mirror-mirror
